critical_rf_chips.name: Semtech SX1280
critical_rf_chips.ndaa_note: US-headquartered fabless semiconductor. Wafers fabricated at TSMC (Taiwan) — see TSMC dependency.
critical_rf_chips.application: ExpressLRS 2.4 GHz — the dominant drone control link protocol
critical_rf_chips.criticality: CRITICAL — every 2.4 GHz ELRS device (TX module, receiver, radio) uses this chip
critical_rf_chips.fab_location: TSMC (Taiwan) — Fab 6 and Fab 11 confirmed by Semtech PCN-000540 (2019)
critical_rf_chips.supply_risk: MEDIUM — single-source IC from Semtech, fabricated at TSMC. Taiwan disruption scenario affects all ELRS hardware.

critical_rf_chips.name: Semtech SX1276/77/78/79
critical_rf_chips.application: ExpressLRS 868/915 MHz — long-range sub-GHz drone control
critical_rf_chips.criticality: HIGH — all 900 MHz ELRS devices use this chip family
critical_rf_chips.supply_risk: MEDIUM — same TSMC dependency as SX1280

critical_rf_chips.name: Texas Instruments CC2500
critical_rf_chips.application: FrSky D16/ACCST protocol. Also in multi-protocol modules (JP4-in-1).
critical_rf_chips.criticality: MEDIUM — legacy protocol, declining usage as ELRS grows
critical_rf_chips.supply_risk: LOW — mature TI product, multiple fab sources

critical_rf_chips.name: Nordic Semiconductor nRF24L01
critical_rf_chips.application: Multi-protocol modules. Various legacy RC protocols.
critical_rf_chips.criticality: LOW — legacy, mostly in JP4-in-1 multi-protocol modules

critical_mcus.name: Espressif ESP32 / ESP8285
critical_mcus.application: ELRS receivers and TX modules — WiFi configuration, OTA firmware updates
critical_mcus.criticality: HIGH — most ELRS receivers use ESP8285 or ESP32 for WiFi update capability
critical_mcus.fab_location: TSMC (Taiwan) for ESP32; various for ESP8285
critical_mcus.supply_risk: MEDIUM — Chinese company, but fabbed at TSMC. Design is Chinese IP.
critical_mcus.ndaa_concern: Espressif is a Shanghai-based company. ESP32 in ELRS devices means even 'open-source' ELRS has Chinese MCU dependency.

critical_mcus.name: STMicroelectronics STM32 family
critical_mcus.application: Radio MCUs (RadioMaster, Jumper), some ELRS receivers, flight controllers
critical_mcus.criticality: CRITICAL — EdgeTX firmware runs on STM32. All serious radios and many receivers use STM32.
critical_mcus.supply_risk: LOW-MEDIUM — European/allied semiconductor, multiple fabs. 2021-2023 chip shortage caused 52-week lead times but has normalized.

firmware_ecosystem.name: ExpressLRS
firmware_ecosystem.strategic_significance: Open-source protocol eliminates vendor lock-in. Any manufacturer can build ELRS hardware. But all current manufacturers are Chinese except ImmersionRC/Orqa.

firmware_ecosystem.name: EdgeTX
firmware_ecosystem.application: Radio transmitter firmware — runs on RadioMaster, Jumper, FrSky hardware
firmware_ecosystem.strategic_significance: Open-source radio firmware. Decouples firmware from hardware vendor. Enables NDAA-compliant hardware to run same software.

firmware_ecosystem.name: TBS Crossfire / CRSF
firmware_ecosystem.protocol_adoption: CRSF serial protocol adopted by Betaflight, ELRS, ArduPilot as de facto standard for FC-to-receiver communication
firmware_ecosystem.strategic_note: Proprietary protocol from Hong Kong company. CRSF protocol itself is widely used even by ELRS — the serial communication layer is TBS-originated.

firmware_ecosystem.name: DJI RC Protocol
firmware_ecosystem.application: DJI FPV system, DJI O3/O4 Air Unit, DJI Avata/Neo
firmware_ecosystem.market_share_estimate: Dominant in consumer FPV video link. Separate from control link in most builds.
firmware_ecosystem.strategic_note: DJI's FPV system is video-only for custom builds (control via ELRS/Crossfire). DJI's own drones use fully proprietary C2+video link.

semtech_corporate.name: Semtech Corporation
semtech_corporate.strategic_significance: Semtech's LoRa platform (SX1276/SX1280) is the silicon foundation of the entire ELRS ecosystem. Single-source IC — no second-source LoRa transceiver exists from any other vendor. A Semtech supply disruption would halt all ELRS hardware production globally.
semtech_corporate.acquired_sierra_wireless: 2023 — $1.2B acquisition added IoT/cellular to LoRa portfolio

tsmc_dependency.risk_scenario: Taiwan contingency — TSMC disruption cascades to all ELRS hardware production
tsmc_dependency.mitigation: None at chip level. ELRS protocol is open-source so alternative RF silicon could theoretically be supported, but no LoRa-equivalent exists.
tsmc_dependency.cross_reference: Same TSMC dependency affects all companion computer SoCs (Jetson, QRB5165) — see PIE flag on Taiwan scenario.
